summary_en: Tender offer by NOVA Holdings closed May 28, 2026 with 624,100 shares acquired (minimum threshold met). NOVA Holdings to become parent company and largest shareholder with >50% voting control from June 4, 2026 (settlement date). ICP (current 33.35% holder, NOVA's parent) to transfer all holdings to NOVA via off-market transaction on same date, becoming indirect parent with >50% voting control. NOVA operates English conversation, cram schools, childcare, study abroad, sports and publishing businesses.
title_en: Notice of Results of Tender Offer for Company Shares by NOVA Holdings Co., Ltd. and Changes in Parent Company, Other Related Companies, Major Shareholders, and Lead Shareholder
translation_en_brief: On May 29, 2026, Manabi-aid Co., Ltd. announced the successful close of a tender offer for its common shares conducted by NOVA Holdings Co., Ltd., which ran from April 27 through May 28, 2026. A total of 624,100 shares were tendered—meeting the minimum threshold for completion. As a result of the settlement to occur on June 4, 2026, NOVA Holdings will become the parent company, major shareholder, and lead shareholder of Manabi-aid, holding over 50% of voting rights. Concurrently, Inayoshi Capital Partners (ICP)—which currently owns 33.35% (1,138,300 shares) of Manabi-aid and is NOVA's parent company—will transfer all its holdings to NOVA via off-market transaction on the same settlement date, transitioning to become an indirect parent company of Manabi-aid with over 50% voting control through NOVA. NOVA Holdings operates English conversation schools, cram schools, childcare and early education services, study-abroad programs, sports businesses, and publishing. ICP is the asset-management vehicle of Masaki Inaoishi (100% shareholder), who serves as representative director of NOVA.
